title: Simple Desk with Style – Metric
summary: This desk provides a great workspace in a compact package.
description: This desk provides a great workspace in a compact package. Thanks to the folding lid, you can leave your laptop and working materials in place on the desk, but cover them up when they’re not in use. On top of being functional, the desk also offers a unique look that’s surprisingly easy to create.

materials.name: Full-inset, partial-wrap hinges for 19mm cabinet door

steps.title: Cut Legs to Size
steps.body: Cut two Back Legs to length from a 19x89mm board, as shown in the cutting diagram. Then cut two Front Legs to length. Note that both ends of the Front Legs are cut at parallel 10° angles. Then, use a quart can to lay out the radius on the Front Legs, and cut it to shape with a jig saw.

steps.title: Cut a Pair of Upper Rails
steps.body: Cut two Upper Side Rails from a 19x184 board. Not that one end is cut at a 10° angle. Then, with your pocket-hole jig set up for 19mm material, drill pocket holes where shown.

steps.title: Cut a Pair of Lower Rails
steps.body: Cut two Lower Side Rails from a 19x89mm board. Not that one end is cut at a 10° angle. With your pocket-hole jig set up for 19mm material, drill pocket holes where shown.

steps.title: Assemble the Sides
steps.body: Now you can attach the Legs to the Rails using 32mm coarse-thread pocket-hole screws, as shown. Glue pocket-hole plugs into the holes, and then sand the plugs smooth.

steps.title: Make a Desk Top
steps.body: Cut a Desk Top to size from a quarter-sheet of 19mm plywood. With your pocket-hole jig set up for 19mm material, drill pocket holes where shown. Also drill a 50mm hole near the back edge for power cords to pass through. Apply iron-on edgebanding to the front edge of the Top, as well.

steps.title: Add a Back and Back Rail
steps.body: Cut a Back to length from a 19x184 board, and a Back Rail to length from a 19x89mm board, as shown in the cutting diagram. With your pocket-hole jig set up for 19mm material, drill pocket holes where shown.

steps.title: Attach the Back and Desk Top
steps.body: Attach the Back to the Desk Top using 32mm coarse-thread pocket-hole screws, then attach these pieces to one of the side assemblies. Also attach the Back Rail, aligning it with the Lower Side Rail.

steps.title: Make the Lid Parts
steps.body: Cut a Lid to length from a 19x235mm board, as shown in the cutting diagram. Also cut a Lid Front and a Lid Support to length from a 19x64mm board. Then drill pocket holes, as shown, with your pocket-hole jig set up for 19mm material.

steps.title: Bring in Support and a Side
steps.body: Now you can attach the Lid Support using 32mm coarse-thread pocket-hole screws. Then bring in the other side assembly and secure it.

steps.title: Assemble the Lid
steps.body: Attach the Lid Front to the Lid using 32mm coarse-thread pocket-hole screws. Note that the Lid front is set back 13mm from the edge of the Lid.

steps.title: Install the Lid, then Paint
steps.body:
To complete construction of your desk, install a pair of hinges on the Lid, and connect them to the Lid Support. Then you can paint, stain, or finish the desk and put it to work.

**Tip:** Paint with Natural Edges The paint finish on this desk might look complicated, but it’s easy. The secret is to use foam rollers, and go lightly near the edges. Don’t load the roller with a lot of paint, and it won’t seep over the edges. You’ll have to apply several light coats, but the result is worth it. If any paint gets on the edges, just let it dry, and then sand it away. To protect he edges, wipe on a coat of natural oil finish after the paint dries.
